title: Surgeries — Callum Anderson for Buckingham & Bletchley
headings: h3: What are constituency surgeries? And how can I help?

Back My role as your MP Constituency Surgeries Casework Parliament Visits Office Standards Folder: Accountable to you Back My Priorities My Visits Annual Report Bletchley Investment Taskforce Work for me Contact me News and views Upcoming constituency surgeries Below are the dates of my upcoming constituency surgeries. Email my team to make an appointment and find out the exact location - and please note that surgeries have to be booked at least 24 hours ahead of time. Bletchley: Friday 5 June, 12:30-13:30 Winslow: Friday 12 June, 11:30-12:30 Buckingham: Friday 19 June, 15:00-16:00 Bletchley: Friday 26 June, 17:00-18:30 What are constituency surgeries? And how can I help? Constituency surgeries provide people living within the towns, villages and city that make up the Buckingham and Bletchley constituency the opportunity to raise issues of a personal or national nature with me directly. I can offer assistance with matters that Parliament and central government are responsible for. This will include government departments (e.g. Department for Work and Pensions, or the Home Office). I can also write to senior officials or Ministers in government departments and agencies. My team and I will try to help in any way that we can, but some issues fall outside of the remit of a Member of Parliament. If your case relates to matters which either Milton Keynes City Council or Buckinghamshire Council are responsible for I encourage you to seek support from your elected ward councillors in the first instance – this will include housing, parking, waste collection and planning. I’m afraid that I cannot help you fill an application form for benefits or housing, or with legal problems. I also cannot write to private individuals (for example, your neighbours) on your behalf. Strict parliamentary protocol dictates that MPs can only assist their own constituents . Please check that your postcode falls within the Buckingham and Bletchley constituency before coming along . You can do so here. Here are some other useful telephone numbers: Emergency services (urgent) – 999 Medical help (non-urgent) – 111 Police (non-urgent) – 101 National Domestic Abuse Helpline (Refuge) – 0808 2000 247 Samaritans – 116 123 Citizens Advice – 0344 477 0808 Street Link (National Homeless Support Helpline) – 0300 500 0914 Shelter - 0808 800 4444.
